repo hygiene · the last cosmetic items

GitHub About stamp + overcut/ cleanup

Two low-severity findings from the public-surface assessment: the GitHub About blurb had no drift-guard (correct today, but manual), and a dead experiment sat tracked-but-gitignored.

① About auto-stamp — closes the structural gap

The About blurb (… 113 skills, 37 agents, 212 hooks …) is a GitHub repo setting, not a tracked file — so stamp-counts.sh can't touch it and it drifts on the next count change. New tool derives it from the already-stamped README markers:

$ node scripts/stamp-github-about.mjs --check
stamp-github-about: in sync (yonatangross/orchestkit)   # exit 0

$ node scripts/stamp-github-about.mjs             # PATCH About to match README
sourcethe canonical <!--ork:skills-->113 / agents / hooks markers in README.md (kept in sync by stamp-counts.sh)
wiringapply needs administration: write — the release-bot app has only contents: write, so auto-run is a follow-up (grant the app perm, or run manually on count changes)

② overcut/ — untracked

was9 files tracked, yet listed in .gitignore:11, and referenced nowhere (verified: 0 grep hits) — a dead standalone agentic-workflow experiment
nowgit rm -r --cached overcut/ — stops tracking, files kept on disk; already gitignored so it was never in the plugin/npm package

Both are cosmetic hygiene — nothing was broken. About/Packages/Releases already track HEAD; this just stops the one manual surface from drifting and clears one orphan.
